skill.name: image
skill.description: Guides AI image creation, editing, mockups, banners, social graphics, OG images, and web image optimization for marketing workflows.
skill.summary: Create and optimize marketing images with model guidance, prompt patterns, sizing rules, and production workflows.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high-risk patterns, but review found Markdown guidance, examples, and evaluation text rather than executable malware. The command examples and documentation links are legitimate for image optimization and marketing workflow guidance, with no prompt injection or credential exfiltration evidence found.

low_findings.title: Benign Command Examples in Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill includes example commands for screenshot capture, WebP conversion, JPEG optimization, and page image inspection. These are user-facing Markdown examples for image workflows, not hidden execution logic, but they can affect local files if copied and run without review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in fenced documentation and reference common image tools. There is no evidence that the skill auto-executes them or constructs shell commands from user input.

low_findings.title: Benign Documentation Links and Placeholder URLs
low_findings.description: The skill links to image model documentation, pricing pages, and placeholder site URLs for OG image examples. These network indicators do not show data exfiltration or calls to unknown endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are public documentation, pricing links, or examples using yoursite.com. No credential collection, webhook endpoint, or unauthorized upload target is present.

low_findings.title: Scanner False Positives From Marketing Image Terminology
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography and screen-capture detections map to words like Flux, GPT Image, screenshots, and upload in marketing guidance and eval assertions. They are not cryptographic routines or covert screen capture behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirmed these lines discuss image tools, real screenshots, and model limitations. They do not contain crypto APIs, capture automation, or upload code.

low_findings.title: Limited Local Context File Read
low_findings.description: The skill asks the agent to read product marketing context files if they exist. This is a scoped project-context read for task quality, but users should avoid placing secrets in those files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read is explicit, narrow, and tied to marketing context. It is still a minor privacy consideration because local context files may contain sensitive business information.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands Are Presented as User-Visible Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill shows shell commands for image conversion and inspection. They are not executed by the skill, but command examples should be reviewed before use on production assets.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are common image workflow utilities and a curl example against a placeholder URL. The risk is operational misuse, not malicious automation.

content.value_statement: Marketing teams need images that fit each channel and load quickly. This skill guides prompt design, model choice, mockup workflows, and optimization steps for production-ready visuals.
actual_capabilities: Selects suitable tools for AI generation, image editing, design templates, screenshots, stock photos, and optimization.
Provides structured prompt patterns for blog heroes, social graphics, product lifestyle shots, profile banners, and listing visuals.
Recommends image dimensions and aspect ratios for major social, profile, store, and preview placements.
Guides product mockup workflows using real screenshots, device frames, annotations, and overlays.
Explains web image formats, compression targets, lazy loading, explicit dimensions, CDN use, and alt text.
Covers dynamic OG image approaches using Vercel OG, Satori, and Cloudinary templates.
limitations: It does not generate images by itself unless the host AI tool provides an image generation capability.
It cannot verify current model pricing or platform specifications without fresh documentation checks.
It should not replace a designer for final brand systems, logos, or pixel-perfect creative review.
Command examples require installed local tools and should be reviewed before running on production files.

use_cases.title: Launch Visuals for Founders
use_cases.description: Create hero images, Product Hunt gallery visuals, profile banners, and social previews with consistent dimensions and brand treatment.

use_cases.title: Reusable Campaign Assets
use_cases.description: Plan one visual concept and adapt it across Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, and email placements.

use_cases.title: Performance-Friendly Website Images
use_cases.description: Choose formats, resize images, compress assets, set layout dimensions, and prepare OG image metadata for faster pages.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Blog Hero
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one marketing image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a blog hero image concept for this topic: [topic]. Recommend the best generation model, exact dimensions, and a finished prompt using subject, setting, style, lighting, composition, and technical specs.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt One Image Across Social Channels
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this visual concept into image specs for Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Instagram feed, Instagram Stories, and Facebook: [concept]. Include dimensions, cropping notes, and text overlay guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Product Mockup Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for real product visuals.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a product mockup for [product]. Use real screenshots, device framing, annotations, and export sizes. Explain which parts should use design tools instead of AI generation.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Scalable OG Image System
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a dynamic OG image workflow for [site or blog]. Include template structure, metadata fields, image dimensions, platform tags, optimization steps, and quality checks.

output_examples.input: I need a hero image for a blog post about email deliverability.
output_examples.output: Recommended approach: generate a 1200x630 blog hero and reuse it as the OG image. Concept: glowing messages moving through a clean routing system, with clear delivery signals and soft commercial lighting. Use Flux or Gemini for photorealism, then export WebP under 200KB with a JPEG fallback.

output_examples.input: Generate an image of our app dashboard.
output_examples.output: Do not use AI generation for the dashboard itself because models invent interface details. Capture a real 2x screenshot, place it in a laptop or browser frame, add concise callouts, and crop versions for the target channels.

output_examples.input: We publish two blog posts each week and need OG images automatically.
output_examples.output: Use a dynamic OG image template. Put the post title, category, author, and brand elements into a fixed 1200x630 layout, then generate unique previews during publishing.

best_practices: Start with the placement and dimensions before choosing a model or design tool.
Use real product screenshots for interface visuals and reserve AI generation for scenes, concepts, and backgrounds.
Compress, resize, and test images at their real display size before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not ask AI models to invent exact product UI screenshots.
Do not create text-heavy images unless the chosen model handles text reliably or text is added later.
Do not ship large source images directly to web pages without resizing and compression.

faq.question: Can this skill generate images directly?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and prompts. Direct generation depends on whether Claude, Codex, Claude Code, or another connected tool has image generation access.

faq.question: Which image model should I use for marketing assets?
faq.answer: Use the model that fits the task. Flux and Gemini suit many hero images, Ideogram is better for text, and design tools suit templates.

faq.question: Should I use AI for product screenshots?
faq.answer: No. Capture the real product UI, then frame, annotate, crop, and optimize it for each placement.

faq.question: What dimensions should I use for OG images?
faq.answer: The skill recommends 1200x630 for standard OG and social preview images.

faq.question: Does the skill cover image optimization?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers WebP, AVIF, JPEG, PNG, SVG, compression targets, lazy loading, explicit dimensions, CDNs, and alt text.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with low risk. The reviewed issues are documentation examples and public links, not hidden execution or data exfiltration.
